title: Table Borders
content:
### HTML Table Borders

---

### Default Table

By default, HTML tables have **no borders**. You add them with CSS.

---

### Adding Borders

```html
<style>
  table, th, td {
    border: 1px solid black;
  }
</style>
```

---

### Collapsed Borders

To avoid double borders, use `border-collapse`:

```html
<style>
  table {
    border-collapse: collapse;
  }
  th, td {
    border: 1px solid black;
  }
</style>
```

---

### Style Comparison

| Property | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Default (no border) | No lines |
| `border: 1px solid black` | Double borders |
| `border-collapse: collapse` | Single borders |
| `border-collapse: separate` | Double borders (default) |

---

### Rounding Table Borders

```html
<style>
  table {
    border-collapse: collapse;
    border-radius: 10px;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
</style>
```

---

### Dotted / Dashed Borders

```html
<style>
  th, td {
    border: 2px dashed #333;
  }
</style>
```

Other styles: `dotted`, `dashed`, `solid`, `double`, `groove`, `ridge`, `inset`, `outset`.

---

> **Best Practice:** Always use `border-collapse: collapse` for cleaner table appearance. Use CSS, not the deprecated `border` attribute on `<table>`.
codeExample:
Name Age
Alice 25
Bob 30
